rulings.Case Title: SHEREEN PINTO et al VS. M. GAEHWILER CONSTRUCTION,INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Barnecut Roofing, Inc. And Gary Degliantoni'S Motion For Summary Adjudication (Control Date)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 2, 2015, Line 4, Defendants Barnecut Roofing, Inc. And Gary Degliantoni's Motion For Summary Adjudication. Denied as to the sixth cause of action and granted as to the request for punitive damages. Plainitffs have presented evidence of an agreement that Defendants would install two plies of Bituthane to prevent leaks (See Declaration of James Joyce 95: 17-25.) A trier of fact could find that an agreement existed between the parties to install two plies of Biththane and Defendants' nonperformance constituted a breach. Therefore, there is a triable issue of material fact precluding summary adjudication of the sixth cause of action for willful misconduct/conscious disregard. Defendants have met their burden of production and plaintiffs have not presented any evidence establishing a triable issue regarding punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. THE DOCTORS' COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Pltf.'S Motion For Stay Pending Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 2, 2015, Line 8, Plaintiff's Motion For Stay Pending Appeal. The motion is denied. "A trial judge has no inherent power to give a stay pending appeal, except to the extent permitted by statute." ( People v. Superior Court. (1992) 2 Cal.App.4th 675, 686). Pursuant to CCP 917.1, Mt. Tam must post an undertaking to stay execution of the appeal because defendant TDC obtained a money judgment. The court could stay execution for a limited time per CCP 918, but Mt. Tam does not demonstrate good cause to extend the limited stay. Mt. Tam's citation to Davis v. Custom Component Switches, Inc. (1970) 13 Cal.App.3d 21, 27, and Estate of Murphy (1971) 16 Cal.App.3d 564, 568, are off point. Those authorities explain a reviewing court's ability to stay execution of a judgment per CCP 923. Mt. Tam's reference to CCP 918.5, which was improperly raised for the first time in the reply brief, is inapposite because that statute allows a stay where the judgment creditor and debtor have a disputed claim in another case. Mt. Tam is not a party to case CGC-15-544542. There is no authority to reduce the bond amount. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MISTI JOHNSON VS. HONG ZHOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 2, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANTS HONG ZHOU AND NANCY ZHOU'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Moving party shall provide copies of the discovery responses by plaintiff which contain objections by sending a pdf image of said discovery to cgeerhart@gmail.com. The Court tentatively is inclined to deny sanctions because the notice is defective under CCP sec. 2023.040 (does not state against whom sanctions are sought). The remaining issue of whether plaintiff's objections are preserved in the present circumstances, and whether a separate motion by plaintiff would be required, will be the subject of argument at the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP CHANDLER ET AL VS. THOMAS WERDEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Thomas Werdel To Transfer Case To The Probate Department
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 2, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT THOMAS WERDEL'S Motion To Transfer Case To The Probate Department. Granted. The case is transferred to the Probate Department for all purposes. Probate Code 17000(a) provides that the Probate Department has "exclusive jurisdiction of proceedings concerning the internal affairs of trusts." All of the allegations against Thomas Werdel concern the internal affairs of the Alberta W. Chandler Trust. Further, Probate Code 17000(b) provides that the Probate Department has concurrent jurisdiction over "other actions and proceedings involving trustees and third persons." Accordingly, the allegations that Defendant Bruce Chandler interfered with the administration of the trust may also be resolved by the Probate Department. The request for sanctions is denied. CCP 396b(b) pertains to a change of venue, not to a change of departments within a superior court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
